title: Signal incorrectly detected as multi-driven
body:
The following code:

```
interface test_if #(parameter int AA = 2, BB=5);

   logic [AA-1 : 0]     a;
   logic [BB-1 : 0]     b;
   logic                c;
   logic                d;

   modport slave (input  a,
                  input  b,
                  input  c,
                  input  d);

   modport master (output a,
                   output b,
                   output c,
                   output d);

endinterface : test_if

module test
  (input logic [28:0]  a,
   output logic [28:0] b);

   always_comb begin
      b = a;
   end
endmodule

module multi_driven
  (
   input logic [20-1 : 0] data_in,
   output logic [20-1 : 0] data_out,
   test_if.slave  test_if_in,
   test_if.master test_if_out
   );

   test test_inst
     (
      .a({data_in,
          test_if_in.a,
          test_if_in.b,
          test_if_in.c,
          test_if_in.d}),
      .b({data_out,
          test_if_out.a,
          test_if_out.b,
          test_if_out.c,
          test_if_out.d}));

endmodule;
```

Gives the following warnings:

```
verilator --no-timing --lint-only -Wall multi_driven.sv
%Warning-DECLFILENAME: multi_driven.sv:1:11: Filename 'multi_driven' does not match IFACE name: 'test_if'
    1 | interface test_if #(parameter int AA = 2, BB=5);
      |           ^~~~~~~
                       ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/DECLFILENAME?v=5.010
                       ... Use "/* verilator lint_off DECLFILENAME */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: multi_driven.sv:32:28: Bits [19:0] of signal 'data_out' have multiple combinational drivers
                      multi_driven.sv:44:8: ... Location of first driver
   44 |       .b({data_out,
      |        ^
                      multi_driven.sv:44:8: ... Location of other driver
   44 |       .b({data_out,
      |        ^
                      multi_driven.sv:32:28: ... Only the first driver will be respected
%Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s)
```

This signal doesn't seem to be multi-driven to me and moving `data_out` around in the concatenation makes the warning disappear.
